product.problem: Build an API that stores submitted records durably.
goals: Accept records and make them queryable
core_flows: A client submits a record and later queries it
data_model_draft: Record with id, payload, and created timestamp
success_criteria: Records can be created and fetched

architecture: Persist records in PostgreSQL behind a repository interface.
data_flow: Requests validate input, write to storage, and return persisted records.
verification: Integration tests cover create and fetch.

clarifying_question_disposition.rationale: The target runtime question is resolved by the selected v1 runtime decision.
The approved v1 scope excludes protocol and server compatibility.
clarifying_question_disposition.non_goal: No Redis protocol compatibility and no network server in v1

triggers: Gate B selects a database dependency with deployment and maintenance impact.

candidates.title: PostgreSQL documentation
candidates.summary: Primary documentation for the selected relational database.
candidates.used_for: Grounded the durable storage choice and repository boundary.
candidates.rationale: PostgreSQL fits durable relational storage while keeping the v1 API implementation conventional.

selected_patterns.pattern: Use a repository boundary around PostgreSQL persistence.
selected_patterns.rationale: The boundary keeps HTTP handlers independent from database-specific details.

evidence.title: Fixture scope
evidence.used_for: Confirmed durable API storage is in scope and an external queue is out of scope.

evidence.title: PostgreSQL Documentation
evidence.used_for: Grounded the PostgreSQL durable storage choice used by the Gate B implementation plan.

approval_audit.approval_note: Approved fixture intentionally references a missing reconnaissance source.
